description: Your SOC has noticed an increase in distributed SSH brute-force attacks, where a single threat actor uses a large botnet from the same /24 subnet to avoid per-IP lockout thresholds. Using Splunk (http://localhost:8000, admin/SplunkAdmin1!), create an analytical report and an operational dashboard to detect this activity. Generate a report named 'Subnet_Attack_Aggregation' based on the 'security_logs' index that identifies failed SSH logins, aggregates data by /24 subnet to calculate total failures and distinct attacking IP counts, determines the percentage of total global attack traffic each subnet represents, and filters for subnets with more than one distinct attacking IP. Create a dashboard named 'Distributed_Brute_Force_Monitoring' featuring a table of these aggregation results and a timechart showing failed logins over time split by subnet.
